   178→func isFullyEnabled() (enabled bool, agentDesc string, configPath string) {

   181→	if err != nil || !s.Enabled {
   182→		return false, "", ""
   183→	}

   185→	// Check any agent hooks installed (not just Claude Code — works with Gemini too)
   186→	installedAgents := GetAgentsWithHooksInstalled()
   187→	if len(installedAgents) == 0 {
   188→		return false, "", ""
   189→	}

   192→	if !strategy.IsGitHookInstalled() {

   195→
   196→	// Check .entire directory exists
   197→	if !checkEntireDirExists() {

   200→
   201→	// Determine agent description from first installed agent
   202→	desc := string(installedAgents[0]) // fallback to agent name
   203→	if ag, err := agent.Get(installedAgents[0]); err == nil {
   204→		desc = string(ag.Type())
   205→	}
   206→
   207→	// Determine config path - check if local settings exists, otherwise show project settings
   208→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)

   221→// runEnableWithStrategy enables Entire with a specified strategy (non-interactive).
   222→// The selectedStrategy can be either a display name (manual-commit, auto-commit)
   223→// or an internal name (manual-commit, auto-commit).
   224→func runEnableWithStrategy(w io.Writer, selectedStrategy string, localDev, _, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   225→	// Map the strategy to internal name if it's a display name
   226→	internalStrategy := selectedStrategy
   227→	if mapped, ok := strategyDisplayToInternal[selectedStrategy]; ok {
   228→		internalStrategy = mapped
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	// Validate the strategy exists
   232→	strat, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy)
   233→	if err != nil {
   234→		return fmt.Errorf("unknown strategy: %s (use manual-commit or auto-commit)", selectedStrategy)
   235→	}
   236→
   237→	// Detect or select agent
   238→	ag, err := detectOrSelectAgent(w)
   239→	if err != nil {
   240→		return fmt.Errorf("agent selection failed: %w", err)
   241→	}
   242→
   243→	// Setup agent hooks
   244→	if _, err := setupAgentHooks(ag, localDev, forceHooks); err != nil {
   245→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup %s hooks: %w", ag.Type(), err)
   246→	}
   247→
   248→	// Setup .entire directory
   249→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   250→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   251→	}
   252→
   253→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   254→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   255→	if err != nil {
   256→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   257→		settings = &EntireSettings{}
   258→	}
   259→	// Update the specific fields
   260→	settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   261→	settings.LocalDev = localDev
   262→	settings.Enabled = true
   263→
   264→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   265→	if skipPushSessions {
   266→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   267→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   268→		}
   269→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   270→	}
   271→
   272→	// Handle telemetry for non-interactive mode
   273→	// Note: if telemetry is nil (not configured), it defaults to disabled
   274→	if !telemetry || os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   275→		f := false
   276→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   277→	}
   278→
   279→	// Determine which settings file to write to
   280→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   281→	if err != nil {
   282→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   283→	}
   284→	shouldUseLocal, showNotification := determineSettingsTarget(entireDirAbs, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings)
   285→
   286→	if showNotification {
   287→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Info: Project settings exist. Saving to settings.local.json instead.")
   288→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Use --project to update the project settings file.")
   289→	}
   290→
   291→	configDisplay := configDisplayProject
   292→	if shouldUseLocal {
   293→		if err := SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings); err != nil {
   294→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save local settings: %w", err)
   295→		}
   296→		configDisplay = configDisplayLocal
   297→	} else {
   298→		if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   299→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   300→		}
   301→	}
   302→
   303→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   304→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   305→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   306→	}
   307→	strategy.CheckAndWarnHookManagers(w)
   308→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "✓ Hooks installed")
   309→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplay)
   310→
   311→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements
   312→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   313→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   314→	}
   315→
   316→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   317→
   318→	return nil
   319→}

   321→// runEnableInteractive runs the interactive enable flow.
   322→func runEnableInteractive(w io.Writer, localDev, _, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   323→	// Check if already fully enabled — show summary and return early.
   324→	// Skip early return if any configuration flags are set (user wants to reconfigure).
   325→	hasConfigFlags := forceHooks || skipPushSessions || !telemetry || useLocalSettings || useProjectSettings || localDev
   326→	if !hasConfigFlags {
   327→		if fullyEnabled, agentDesc, configPath := isFullyEnabled(); fullyEnabled {
   328→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "Already enabled. Everything looks good.")
   329→			fmt.Fprintln(w)
   330→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Agent: %s\n", agentDesc)
   331→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Config: %s\n", configPath)
   332→			return nil
   333→		}
   334→	}
   335→
   336→	// Detect or select agent
   337→	ag, err := detectOrSelectAgent(w)
   338→	if err != nil {
   339→		return fmt.Errorf("agent selection failed: %w", err)
   340→	}
   341→
   342→	// Setup agent hooks
   343→	if _, err := setupAgentHooks(ag, localDev, forceHooks); err != nil {
   344→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup %s hooks: %w", ag.Type(), err)
   345→	}
   346→
   347→	// Setup .entire directory
   348→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   349→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   350→	}
   351→
   352→	// Use the default strategy (manual-commit)
   353→	internalStrategy := strategy.DefaultStrategyName
   354→
   355→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   356→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   357→	if err != nil {
   358→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   359→		settings = &EntireSettings{}
   360→	}
   361→	// Update the specific fields
   362→	settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   363→	settings.LocalDev = localDev
   364→	settings.Enabled = true
   365→
   366→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   367→	if skipPushSessions {
   368→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   369→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   370→		}
   371→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   372→	}
   373→
   374→	// Determine which settings file to write to
   375→	// First run always creates settings.json (no prompt)
   376→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   377→	if err != nil {
   378→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   379→	}
   380→	shouldUseLocal, showNotification := determineSettingsTarget(entireDirAbs, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings)
   381→
   382→	if showNotification {
   383→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Info: Project settings exist. Saving to settings.local.json instead.")
   384→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Use --project to update the project settings file.")
   385→	}
   386→
   387→	// Helper to save settings to the appropriate file
   388→	saveSettings := func() error {
   389→		if shouldUseLocal {
   390→			return SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings)
   391→		}
   392→		return SaveEntireSettings(settings)
   393→	}
   394→
   395→	// Save settings before telemetry prompt so config is persisted even if the user cancels
   396→	if err := saveSettings(); err != nil {
   397→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   401→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   402→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   403→	}
   404→	strategy.CheckAndWarnHookManagers(w)
   405→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "✓ Hooks installed")
   406→
   407→	configDisplay := configDisplayProject
   408→	if shouldUseLocal {
   409→		configDisplay = configDisplayLocal
   410→	}
   411→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplay)
   412→
   413→	// Ask about telemetry consent (only if not already asked)
   414→	fmt.Fprintln(w)
   415→	if err := promptTelemetryConsent(settings, telemetry); err != nil {
   416→		return fmt.Errorf("telemetry consent: %w", err)
   417→	}
   418→	// Save again to persist telemetry choice
   419→	if err := saveSettings(); err != nil {
   420→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   421→	}
   422→
   423→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements
   424→	strat, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy)
   425→	if err != nil {
   426→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get strategy: %w", err)
   427→	}
   428→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {

   453→func runDisable(w io.Writer, useProjectSettings bool) error {

   455→	if err != nil {
   456→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load settings: %w", err)
   457→	}
   458→
   459→	settings.Enabled = false

   480→// checkDisabledGuard checks if Entire is disabled and prints a message if so.
   481→// Returns true if the caller should exit (i.e., Entire is disabled).
   482→// On error reading settings, defaults to enabled (returns false).
   483→func checkDisabledGuard(w io.Writer) bool {
   484→	enabled, err := IsEnabled()
   485→	if err != nil {
   486→		// Default to enabled on error
   487→		return false

   512→// detectOrSelectAgent tries to auto-detect an agent, or prompts the user to select one.
   513→// Returns the detected/selected agent and any error.
   514→// If no agent is detected and no TTY is available, falls back to the default agent.
   515→func detectOrSelectAgent(w io.Writer) (agent.Agent, error) {
   516→	// Try auto-detection first
   517→	ag, err := agent.Detect()
   518→	if err == nil {
   519→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "Detected agent: %s\n\n", ag.Type())
   520→		return ag, nil
   521→	}
   522→
   523→	// No agent detected - check if we can prompt interactively
   524→	if !canPromptInteractively() {
   525→		// No TTY available (e.g., running in CI or tests) - fall back to default agent
   526→		defaultAgent := agent.Default()
   527→		if defaultAgent == nil {
   528→			return nil, errors.New("no default agent available")
   529→		}
   530→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s (use --agent to change)\n\n", defaultAgent.Type())
   531→		return defaultAgent, nil
   532→	}
   533→
   534→	// Show message and prompt for selection
   535→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "No agent configuration detected (e.g., .claude or .gemini directory).")
   536→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "This is normal - some agents don't require a config directory.")
   537→	fmt.Fprintln(w)
   538→
   539→	// Build options from registered agents
   540→	agentNames := agent.List()
   541→	options := make([]huh.Option[string], 0, len(agentNames))
   542→	for _, name := range agentNames {
   543→		ag, err := agent.Get(name)
   544→		if err != nil {
   545→			continue
   546→		}
   547→		// Only show agents that support hooks
   548→		if _, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport); !ok {
   549→			continue
   550→		}
   551→		label := string(ag.Type())
   552→		if name == agent.DefaultAgentName {
   553→			label += " (default)"
   554→		}
   555→		options = append(options, huh.NewOption(label, string(name)))
   556→	}
   557→
   558→	if len(options) == 0 {
   559→		return nil, errors.New("no agents with hook support available")
   560→	}
   561→
   562→	var selectedAgentName string
   563→	form := NewAccessibleForm(
   564→		huh.NewGroup(
   565→			huh.NewSelect[string]().
   566→				Title("Which agent are you using?").
   567→				Options(options...).
   568→				Value(&selectedAgentName),
   569→		),
   570→	)
   571→
   572→	if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
   573→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent selection cancelled: %w", err)
   574→	}
   575→
   576→	selectedAgent, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentName(selectedAgentName))
   577→	if err != nil {
   578→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get selected agent: %w", err)
   579→	}
   580→
   581→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nSelected agent: %s\n\n", selectedAgent.Type())
   582→	return selectedAgent, nil
   583→}

   585→// canPromptInteractively checks if we can show interactive prompts.

   599→	return true

   628→// setupAgentHooksNonInteractive sets up hooks for a specific agent non-interactively.
   629→// If strategyName is provided, it sets the strategy; otherwise uses default.
   630→func setupAgentHooksNonInteractive(w io.Writer, ag agent.Agent, strategyName string, localDev, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   631→	agentName := ag.Name()
   632→	// Check if agent supports hooks
   633→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
   634→	if !ok {
   635→		return fmt.Errorf("agent %s does not support hooks", agentName)
   636→	}
   637→
   638→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s\n\n", ag.Type())
   639→
   640→	// Install agent hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   641→	installedHooks, err := hookAgent.InstallHooks(localDev, forceHooks)
   642→	if err != nil {
   643→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install hooks for %s: %w", agentName, err)
   644→	}
   645→
   646→	// Setup .entire directory
   647→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   648→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   649→	}
   650→
   651→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   652→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   653→	if err != nil {
   654→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   655→		settings = &EntireSettings{Strategy: strategy.DefaultStrategyName}
   656→	}
   657→	settings.Enabled = true
   658→	if localDev {
   659→		settings.LocalDev = localDev
   660→	}
   661→
   662→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   663→	if skipPushSessions {
   664→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   665→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   666→		}
   667→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   668→	}
   669→
   670→	// Set strategy if provided
   671→	if strategyName != "" {
   672→		// Map display name to internal name if needed
   673→		internalStrategy := strategyName
   674→		if mapped, ok := strategyDisplayToInternal[strategyName]; ok {
   675→			internalStrategy = mapped
   676→		}
   677→		// Validate the strategy exists
   678→		if _, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy); err != nil {
   679→			return fmt.Errorf("unknown strategy: %s (use manual-commit or auto-commit)", strategyName)
   680→		}
   681→		settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   682→	}
   683→
   684→	// Handle telemetry for non-interactive mode
   685→	// Note: if telemetry is nil (not configured), it defaults to disabled
   686→	if !telemetry || os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   687→		f := false
   688→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   689→	}
   690→
   691→	if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   692→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   693→	}
   694→
   695→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   696→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   697→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   698→	}
   699→	strategy.CheckAndWarnHookManagers(w)
   700→
   701→	if installedHooks == 0 {
   702→		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Hooks for %s already installed", ag.Description())
   703→		if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
   704→			msg += " (Preview)"
   705→		}
   706→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\n", msg)
   707→	} else {
   708→		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Installed %d hooks for %s", installedHooks, ag.Description())
   709→		if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
   710→			msg += " (Preview)"
   711→		}
   712→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\n", msg)
   713→	}
   714→
   715→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplayProject)
   716→
   717→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements (creates entire/checkpoints/v1 branch, etc.)
   718→	strat, err := strategy.Get(settings.Strategy)
   719→	if err != nil {
   720→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get strategy: %w", err)
   721→	}
   722→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   723→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   724→	}
   725→
   726→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   727→
   728→	return nil
   729→}

   835→// shellCompletionComment is the comment preceding the completion line
   836→const shellCompletionComment = "# Entire CLI shell completion"
   837→
   838→// errUnsupportedShell is returned when the user's shell is not supported for completion.
   839→var errUnsupportedShell = errors.New("unsupported shell")
   840→
   841→// shellCompletionTarget returns the rc file path and completion lines for the
   842→// user's current shell.
   843→func shellCompletionTarget() (shellName, rcFile, completionLine string, err error) {
   844→	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   845→	if err != nil {
   846→		return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot determine home directory: %w", err)
   847→	}
   848→
   849→	shell := os.Getenv("SHELL")
   850→	switch {
   851→	case strings.Contains(shell, "zsh"):
   852→		return "Zsh",
   853→			filepath.Join(home, ".zshrc"),
   854→			"autoload -Uz compinit && compinit && source <(entire completion zsh)",
   855→			nil
   856→	case strings.Contains(shell, "bash"):
   857→		bashRC := filepath.Join(home, ".bashrc")
   858→		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(home, ".bash_profile")); err == nil {
   859→			bashRC = filepath.Join(home, ".bash_profile")
   860→		}
   861→		return "Bash",
   862→			bashRC,
   863→			"source <(entire completion bash)",
   864→			nil
   865→	case strings.Contains(shell, "fish"):
   866→		return "Fish",
   867→			filepath.Join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish"),
   868→			"entire completion fish | source",
   869→			nil
   870→	default:
   871→		return "", "", "", errUnsupportedShell
   872→	}
   873→}
   874→
   875→// promptShellCompletion offers to add shell completion to the user's rc file.
   876→// Only prompts if completion is not already configured.
   877→func promptShellCompletion(w io.Writer) error {
   878→	shellName, rcFile, completionLine, err := shellCompletionTarget()
   879→	if err != nil {
   880→		if errors.Is(err, errUnsupportedShell) {
   881→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "Note: Shell completion not available for your shell. Supported: zsh, bash, fish.\n")
   882→			return nil
   883→		}
   884→		return fmt.Errorf("shell completion: %w", err)
   885→	}
   886→
   887→	if isCompletionConfigured(rcFile) {
   888→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Shell completion already configured in %s\n", rcFile)
   889→		return nil
   890→	}
   891→
   892→	var selected string
   893→	form := NewAccessibleForm(
   894→		huh.NewGroup(
   895→			huh.NewSelect[string]().
   896→				Title(fmt.Sprintf("Enable shell completion? (detected: %s)", shellName)).
   897→				Options(
   898→					huh.NewOption("Yes", "yes"),
   899→					huh.NewOption("No", "no"),
   900→				).
   901→				Value(&selected),
   902→		),
   903→	)
   904→
   905→	if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
   906→		//nolint:nilerr // User cancelled - not a fatal error, just skip
   907→		return nil
   908→	}
   909→
   910→	if selected != "yes" {
   911→		return nil
   912→	}
   913→
   914→	if err := appendShellCompletion(rcFile, completionLine); err != nil {
   915→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update %s: %w", rcFile, err)
   916→	}
   917→
   918→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Shell completion added to %s\n", rcFile)
   919→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Restart your shell to activate")
   920→
   921→	return nil
   922→}
   923→
   924→// isCompletionConfigured checks if shell completion is already in the rc file.
   925→func isCompletionConfigured(rcFile string) bool {
   926→	//nolint:gosec // G304: rcFile is constructed from home dir + known filename, not user input
   927→	content, err := os.ReadFile(rcFile)
   928→	if err != nil {
   929→		return false // File doesn't exist or can't read, treat as not configured
   930→	}
   931→	return strings.Contains(string(content), "entire completion")
   932→}
   933→
   934→// appendShellCompletion adds the completion line to the rc file.
   935→func appendShellCompletion(rcFile, completionLine string) error {
   936→	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(rcFile), 0o700); err != nil {
   937→		return fmt.Errorf("creating directory: %w", err)
   938→	}
   939→	//nolint:gosec // G302: Shell rc files need 0644 for user readability
   940→	f, err := os.OpenFile(rcFile, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
   941→	if err != nil {
   942→		return fmt.Errorf("opening file: %w", err)
   943→	}
   944→	defer f.Close()
   945→
   946→	_, err = f.WriteString("\n" + shellCompletionComment + "\n" + completionLine + "\n")
   947→	if err != nil {
   948→		return fmt.Errorf("writing completion: %w", err)
   949→	}
   950→	return nil
   951→}

   996→// runUninstall completely removes Entire from the repository.
   997→func runUninstall(w, errW io.Writer, force bool) error {
   998→	// Check if we're in a git repository
   999→	if _, err := paths.RepoRoot(); err != nil {
  1000→		fmt.Fprintln(errW, "Not a git repository. Nothing to uninstall.")
  1001→		return NewSilentError(errors.New("not a git repository"))
  1002→	}
  1003→
  1004→	// Gather counts for display
  1005→	sessionStateCount := countSessionStates()
  1006→	shadowBranchCount := countShadowBranches()
  1007→	gitHooksInstalled := strategy.IsGitHookInstalled()
  1008→	claudeHooksInstalled := checkClaudeCodeHooksInstalled()
  1009→	geminiHooksInstalled := checkGeminiCLIHooksInstalled()
  1010→	entireDirExists := checkEntireDirExists()
  1011→
  1012→	// Check if there's anything to uninstall
  1013→	if !entireDirExists && !gitHooksInstalled && sessionStateCount == 0 &&
  1014→		shadowBranchCount == 0 && !claudeHooksInstalled && !geminiHooksInstalled {
  1015→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Entire is not installed in this repository.")
  1016→		return nil
  1017→	}
  1018→
  1019→	// Show confirmation prompt unless --force
  1020→	if !force {
  1021→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nThis will completely remove Entire from this repository:")
  1022→		if entireDirExists {
  1023→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - .entire/ directory")
  1024→		}
  1025→		if gitHooksInstalled {
  1026→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Git hooks (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit, pre-push)")
  1027→		}
  1028→		if sessionStateCount > 0 {
  1029→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - Session state files (%d)\n", sessionStateCount)
  1030→		}
  1031→		if shadowBranchCount > 0 {
  1032→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - Shadow branches (%d)\n", shadowBranchCount)
  1033→		}
  1034→		switch {
  1035→		case claudeHooksInstalled && geminiHooksInstalled:
  1036→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Claude Code, Gemini CLI)")
  1037→		case claudeHooksInstalled:
  1038→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Claude Code)")
  1039→		case geminiHooksInstalled:
  1040→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Gemini CLI)")
  1041→		}
  1042→		fmt.Fprintln(w)
  1043→
  1044→		var confirmed bool
  1045→		form := NewAccessibleForm(
  1046→			huh.NewGroup(
  1047→				huh.NewConfirm().
  1048→					Title("Are you sure you want to uninstall Entire?").

The user is asking about how to interact with the `huh` multi-select component in the terminal. Let me check how `huh.NewMultiSelect` works - in the charmbracelet/huh library, multi-select uses:

- **Space** to toggle selection of an item
- **Enter** to confirm the selection

This is standard TUI behavior for multi-select components. The user might be confused because with a single select, you just arrow to an item and press Enter. With multi-select, you need to press Space to check/uncheck items, then Enter to submit.

content.text:
With `huh`'s multi-select in the terminal:

- **Space** — toggles an item on/off (checks/unchecks it)
- **Enter** — confirms your selection and moves on
- **Arrow keys** (or j/k) — navigate between items

So the flow is: navigate to an agent, press Space to select it, navigate to another if you want, press Space again, then press Enter to confirm.

    21→	defer registryMu.Unlock()

    30→	defer registryMu.RUnlock()
    31→

    39→// List returns all registered agent names in sorted order.
    40→func List() []AgentName {

    42→	defer registryMu.RUnlock()

    45→	for name := range registry {

    56→func Detect() (Agent, error) {

   105→func GetByAgentType(agentType AgentType) (Agent, error) {

content.text:
Everything passes — fmt, lint, and all tests (unit + integration). Here's a summary of the changes:

**`cmd/entire/cli/agent/registry.go`:**
- Added `DetectAll()` — iterates agents in sorted name order (via `List()`) and returns all agents whose `DetectPresence()` reports true. This is deterministic unlike the old map iteration.
- Rewrote `Detect()` to use `DetectAll()` and return the first match, keeping the same API for existing callers.

**`cmd/entire/cli/setup.go`:**
- `detectOrSelectAgent` now calls `DetectAll()` instead of `Detect()`
- **Single agent detected** — used directly (same as before)
- **Multiple agents detected** — prints "Detected multiple agents: ..." and falls through to the multi-select prompt with detected agents pre-selected
- **Multiple detected + no TTY** — uses all detected agents instead of picking an arbitrary one
- Detected agents are pre-selected in the multi-select via `opt.Selected(true)`

**`cmd/entire/cli/setup_test.go`:**
- Replaced `TestDetectOrSelectAgent_BothDirectoriesExist_DetectsFirst` with two new tests:
  - `TestDetectOrSelectAgent_BothDirectoriesExist_PromptsUser` — verifies the multi-agent detection message is shown
  - `TestDetectOrSelectAgent_BothDirectoriesExist_NoTTY_UsesAll` — verifies all detected agents are returned when no TTY is available
